Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Coastwise Industrial Co.'s motion to enforce settlement agreement and enter judgment pursuant to C.C.P. 664.6 is denied without prejudice. MOTION TO DISMISS
Off Calendar Nov-15-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
James N. Sinunu of SINUNU BRUNI, LLP (415-362-9700), appearing for defendant / moving party Coastwise Industrial Co., a dissolved corporation
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Esther Shiffer as successor in interest to and as wrongful death heir of James Shiffer, deceased, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT COASTWISE INDUSTRIAL CO., A DISSOLVED CORPORATION'S MOTION TO DISMISS

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Coastwise Industrial Co.'s motion to dismiss for delay in prosecution pursuant to C.C.P. 583.410-583.430 and CRC 3.1342 is denied. Plaintiffs served defendant more than two years after commencing their action for survival and wrongful death. As defendant was substituted in for a Doe defendant, the two-year period began to run on June 30, 2014. Defendant was served more than two years from that date, constituting prima facie grounds for dismissal. However plaintiffs met their burden of submitting a credible excuse for the delay in service and the factors enunciated in CRC 3.1342(e) weigh in favor of denying the motion. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: David A. Disbrow, CSR #7768 (Tel: 415-298-1538) (503/CML)

Off Calendar Jun-10-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Simon S. Grille of BRAYTON PURCELL LLP 415.898.1555, appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Esther Shiffer as successor in interest to and as wrongful death heir of James Shiffer, Deceased; et al.
Ferlin P. Ruiz of TUCKER ELLIS LLP 415.617.2222, appearing for defendant / opposing party Applied Industrial Technologies, Inc.

RULING:
Plaintiffs' Motion To Compel Defendant Applied Industrial Technologies, Inc., To Produce Its Custodian Of Records And Person Most Knowledgeable For Deposition And To Produce Documents At Deposition And Plaintiffs' Request For Sanctions.

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Plaintiffs' motion to compel defendant Applied Industrial Technologies, Inc. to produce its custodian of records and person most knowledgeable for deposition and to produce documents at deposition is granted. Defendant shall produce a deponent(s) and responsive documents no later than June 30, 2015. The deposition and document production are not limited in scope beyond that provided in plaintiffs' deposition notice. The timing of the deposition and document production shall not be dictated by the timing of the depositions of plaintiffs or Darrell Richardson. Plaintiffs demonstrated that the discovery is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence and defendant failed to demonstrate that the scope should be limited to one jobsite and two specific products. Order SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.
Counsel present argument as to the plaintiffs' request for monetary sanctions. (C.C.P. sections 2025.480(j), 2023.010, 2023.030(a).) At the conclusion of argument, the Court took the matter under submission. Each side is to submit a proposed order to the Court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Cathy Meyer of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #11596 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
